license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zgbf3sn
title: Immunoprecipitation assay
author: Ning Zhang , Peng Gao

steps_raw.instruction: To harvest cells in nondenaturing conditions, remove media and rinse cells with ice cold 1×PBS
Remove PBS and add 0.5 ml ice cold cell lysis buffer to each plate(10cm) and incubate on ice for 5 min

native protein: Scrape cells off the plate and transfer to microcentrifuge tubes. Keep on ice. Sonicate on ice 3 times for 5 sec each. [denatured condition: scrape cells off the plate and transfer to 2.5 ml tube, boil for 10 min, then add 4 volumes cell lysis buffer, this is the cell lysate. ]
Native protein : Microcentrifuge for 10 min at 4 ℃,14000g and transfer the supernatant to a new tube. The supernatant is the cell lysates, store at -80℃.
add primary antibody(at the appropriate dilution as recommended in the product datasheet) to 200ul cell lysate at 1mg/ml. incubate with gentle rocking overnight at 4℃.
Add protein A/G agarose beads (10-30ul of 50% bead slurry). Incubate with gentle rocking for 1-3h at 4 ℃.
Microcentrifuge for 30 sec at 4℃. Wash pellet five times with 500ul cell lysis buffer. Keep on ice between washes.
Resuspend the pellet with 20ul SDS sample buffer. Vortex, then microcentrifuge for 30sec.
Heat the sample to 95-100℃ fro 5 min and microcentrifuge for 1min at 14000g.Or, do not heat.
Load the sample on a 4-20% gel for SDS-PAGE.
